summary: Iteration 3 of CC-0089 (Prometheus metrics + ServiceMonitor) addressing external review #2 (gndrmnn). Five test-organisation findings were resolved by: (1) merging cardinality_test.go into collectors_test.go as TestSubReconcilerMetricsHaveNoCRLabels, (2) moving the test-only newCollectorsForTest constructor out of collectors.go into collectors_test.go, (3) deleting contract_test.go (doc-vs-code drift guard) entirely, (4) deleting the AST-based literal-analysis drift guard from instrumentation_test.go, and (5) keeping the internal *collectors methods in collectors.go with a corrected comment because they are forwarded to by every public package-level helper (not test-only). Tests pass (metrics + controller), go vet is clean, and gosec/lint find no issues on the changed files.

tests_checklist.item: Test tier completeness preserved (unit + cardinality drift guard retained)
Cardinality drift guard still enforced as runtime test (TestSubReconcilerMetricsHaveNoCRLabels)

code_quality_checklist.item: Function comments updated to reflect new structure (collectors.go:37-40 and 199-205)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
Test-only code relocated out of production file (newCollectorsForTest)
Cross-package test helper (NewTestRecorder) intentionally retained in production file because Go cannot expose _test.go symbols across packages — a constraint, not a smell

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (panic on duplicate registration)

issues_found.description: Minor 7-line duplication between metrics/collectors.go:112-120 (NewTestRecorder) and metrics/collectors_test.go:28-37 (newCollectorsForTest). Both call newCollectors(), then c.register(reg), and panic with the same message on registration error. newCollectorsForTest could be implemented as `return NewTestRecorder(reg).c` since it lives in the same package and has access to the unexported field. Not a bug, not a regression — just a small DRY opportunity.
issues_found.fix: Replace the body of newCollectorsForTest with `return NewTestRecorder(reg).c` to forward through the public constructor and eliminate the duplicated register+panic block.

suggested_improvements: Consider folding newCollectorsForTest into a one-line wrapper around NewTestRecorder to remove the small block of duplicated registration logic. Cosmetic only; functionality is identical.
next_steps: No blocking changes required. Optional: collapse the duplicated register+panic block as described above before merge.
